D1 Optics L3 Apply Reference

Critical Angle for Total Internal Reflection

tilt the laser inside glass and find the exact angle where it can no longer escape

When light travels from a denser to a rarer medium, beyond a certain angle it cannot refract — it reflects entirely back inside.

Slide θ₁ across the critical angle. The refracted slate ray vanishes and a crimson reflection takes over — exactly the principle that traps light inside an optical fibre.
